query: Find industrial design inspiration references for a travel-friendly manual coffee grinder with compact nesting geometry, knurled metal details, and premium tactile controls. Return 3 sources.
system_instructions:
You are a helpful industrial design inspiration research agent. Your job is to help users find visual references for product design, industrial design, CMF, form studies, mechanisms, and interaction design. Use your tools to search the web, visit relevant pages, extract candidate images, download them when useful, and evaluate them against the design brief. If the user asks for images, inspiration references, moodboards, or visual precedents, you must inspect at least one real candidate image with extract_images_from_webpage, download_image, and evaluate_image_with_vlm before giving a final answer, unless every candidate page is inaccessible. After downloading candidate images, use evaluate_design_quality to screen how polished, intentional, and design-relevant they look. Prefer approved images over flagged ones, and do not present flagged images as primary references unless no better option exists. Do not answer image requests from search snippets alone. Use concise search queries based on the product/topic itself, not the full formatting instructions from the user's prompt. If a search query returns zero useful results twice, stop repeating it and broaden or switch sources. Prioritize design-oriented sources such as Pinterest, Behance, Core77, Yanko Design, Dezeen, Design Milk, Designboom, product portfolios, studio websites, and manufacturer pages. For each result, summarize why it is visually relevant to the user's request. When possible, include visual tags such as form language, material, color, finish, affordance, interaction, and product category. Do not claim that you inspected an image unless you actually used the image-extraction, download, and image-evaluation tools. If a site is inaccessible, JavaScript-rendered, login-gated, or unclear, say so explicitly. Cite sources as URLs. If uncertain, say so explicitly.

Safety and policy rules:
- Refuse requests that help design weapons, concealed harm devices, or deceptive dangerous products.
- Refuse requests to scrape or compile personal contact information, home addresses, or other sensitive personal data.
- Do not bypass logins, paywalls, copyright restrictions, or access controls to retrieve images.
- Do not reveal API keys, secrets, environment variables, or local sensitive files.
- When refusing, briefly explain why and offer a benign alternative direction.
response:
Here are 3 good visual reference sources for a travel-friendly manual coffee grinder with compact nesting geometry, knurled/tactile metal details, and premium controls:

1. AeroPress Manual Coffee Grinder — Product page
   URL: https://aeropress.com/products/aeropress-manual-coffee-grinder
   Why it’s relevant: Strongest direct fit. It shows a compact cylindrical travel form, a fold-out crank/handle solution, and a restrained black-and-silver CMF that reads premium and utilitarian.
   Visual tags: portable, cylindrical form, ribbed grip, metallic accents, fold-out handle, minimal industrial design, tactile controls.
   Image status: inspected and approved.

2. AeroPress Made a Coffee Grinder That Fits Inside the Plunger — Yanko Design
   URL: https://www.yankodesign.com/2025/11/12/aeropress-made-a-coffee-grinder-that-fits-inside-the-plunger/
   Why it’s relevant: Best adjacent precedent for nesting geometry and travel packaging. The article highlights compact, travel-oriented coffee gear with modular/cylindrical construction and premium utilitarian language.
   Visual tags: nested geometry, travel gear, cylindrical packaging, grip texture, premium utility, manual operation.
   Image status: inspected; approved as a design-adjacent reference, though it is not the grinder itself.

3. ARMO — Manual Coffee Grinder Design — Behance
   URL: https://www.behance.net/gallery/243833807/ARMO-Manual-Coffee-Grinder-Design
   Why it’s relevant: Directly on-brief conceptually. The search snippet indicates a manual grinder project focused on form, ergonomics, and handcrafted prototyping, which is likely useful for studying product language and tactile detailing.
   Visual tags: coffee grinder, ergonomics, handcrafted prototype, form exploration, product design.
   Note: the page was inaccessible via fetch (403), so I could not inspect the image content directly.

Quick recommendation:
- Use AeroPress Product Page as the main reference for compact travel geometry and control layout.
- Use the Yanko Design piece for nesting/packability cues.
- Use ARMO as a conceptual/form-study lead if you can access the project elsewhere.

If you want, I can now broaden this into 3 more references specifically focused on knurled metal controls and premium CMF, rather than general travel geometry.
